title: Defining Model Size
concept: Model size is determined by parameters, which are the weights and biases in the embedding, attention, and feed-forward layers.

exercises.prompt: What does the term 'parameters' mean when talking about AI models?

options.text: The length of the prompts you type
The internal adjustable settings (weights and biases) of the model
The speed of the internet connection

exercises.explain: In AI, 'parameters' refers to the adjustable values (weights and biases) that the model learns from data to make decisions.

cards.title: How Big is an AI?
cards.body: When people talk about 'massive' AI models, they aren't talking about physical size or the number of code lines. They are talking about parameters! Let's find out what that means.

cards.title: What is Model Size?
cards.body: Model size measures how big an AI's 'brain' is. It is calculated by adding up all the parameters—the adjustable weights and biases—found in its embedding, attention, and feed-forward layers.

cards.title: The Spaceship Control Panel
cards.body: Think of a neural network like a giant control panel in a spaceship. The parameters are all the dials, knobs, and switches on that panel. The more dials (parameters) you have, the more precise control you have over the ship's flight, but it also takes a much bigger ship to hold them all!

cards.title: Small vs. Large Models
cards.body: A small model like GPT-2 has about 1.5 billion parameters. In contrast, a massive model like GPT-3 has 175 billion parameters. More parameters allow the model to capture more complex patterns, but require much more computer memory to run.

cards.title: Myth: Bigger is Always Better
cards.body: People often think that a model with more parameters is automatically smarter at everything. In reality, a smaller model trained on high-quality data can easily outperform a larger model that was poorly trained. Bigger isn't always better!

cards.title: Comparing Model Sizes
cards.body: We can compare models by looking at their parameter count. Larger parameters mean more capacity, while smaller parameters mean faster speed.

left.text: Fewer parameters. Fits on a phone, runs fast, but knows less details.

right.text: Billions of parameters. Needs massive cloud servers, but handles complex tasks.

exercises.prompt: What are the actual parameters that make up an AI's model size?

options.text: Weights and biases in its layers
The number of lines of Python code
The number of images in the training dataset
The physical size of the computer chip

exercises.explain: Weights and biases are the adjustable values (parameters) located inside the model's layers that are tuned during training.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Parameters are found in multiple layers of a model, including the attention and feed-forward layers.
exercises.explain: Every layer of a transformer model—including embedding, attention, and feed-forward layers—contains parameters that add to the total model size.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of model size.
exercises.sentence: An AI's model size is determined by the total number of its internal adjustable parameters.
exercises.explain: Parameters are the fundamental adjustable settings (weights and biases) that determine the size and capacity of an AI model.

exercises.prompt: Identify the AI that incorrectly defines model size.

options.text: AI A: 'Model size is determined by how many gigabytes of text files we feed into it during training.'
AI B: 'Model size is determined by the total count of weights and biases in the model\'s layers.'
AI C: 'Model size is calculated by adding up all the parameters in the embedding, attention, and feed-forward layers.'

exercises.explain: Prompt A shows a common misunderstanding: model size is defined by the internal parameters (weights and biases) of the neural network, not by the amount of text it reads.

exercises.prompt: You are building a custom AI and need to explain to your team what actually determines your model's size. Which of these is the correct definition?

options.text: The number of words in its training data
The physical size of the hard drive it is saved on
The total number of adjustable weights and biases in its layers
The speed at which the model generates text

exercises.explain: Parameters are the individual settings (weights and biases) inside the model's layers. The total count of these settings across all layers determines the overall model size!
